level_signals: 2+ years of experience in Revenue Operations or related Finance function
Base range $66,189–$94,556 consistent with junior-to-mid individual contributor

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 2+ years of experience in Revenue Operations, Accounts Receivable, Credit & Collections, Compliance, or a related Finance function
You are experienced working with ERP systems such as SAP, Oracle, or NetSuite
You are comfortable working with large datasets and have strong Excel skills
You know how to manage your time, prioritize tasks, and meet deadlines in a fast-moving environment
You communicate clearly and collaborate effectively across teams and stakeholders
You are detail-oriented and care about accuracy and data integrity
You adapt well to change and are comfortable navigating evolving priorities

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You are curious about improving processes and open to using automation or AI tools

scope.surface: Order to Cash (O2C) process — sales order review, customer master data, credit workflows, billing and collections
scope.business_model: B2B and B2C audio streaming subscription
core_responsibilities: Review and support sales orders to ensure accuracy, compliance, and alignment with internal standards
Monitor key processes and service levels, helping the team stay on track and meet agreed timelines
Support manual processes and special projects as business needs evolve
Maintain and improve customer master data across systems, including account setup, updates, and data quality checks
Partner with cross-functional teams to improve workflows and address data issues impacting billing or collections
Support credit processes and workflows using internal tools
Handle urgent cases and escalations, partnering with leadership to resolve issues thoughtfully and efficiently
Contribute to reporting, metrics tracking, and insights that support decision-making
Identify opportunities to improve processes through automation or AI-driven solutions

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated ($66,189–$94,556 base plus equity)
Detailed benefits package disclosed (health insurance, 6-month parental leave, 401k, meal allowance, 23 PTO days, sick leave)
Clear hybrid flexibility (2–3 days/week in office) with work-from-home allowed
Specific team context and cross-functional partners identified (Finance, Sales, Product)
Role at a globally recognized brand with scale and visibility
red_flags: Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned — unclear for non-US-authorized candidates
Base range tops out at ~$94K for a NYC-based role at a large tech company, which may be below market for the city
Role involves significant manual process support which may limit growth opportunities

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement and compensation range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
ambiguities: The nice-to-have for process improvement/automation is phrased as a personality trait ('curious', 'open to') making it borderline cultural_fit vs. domain_knowledge; classified as cultural_fit with soft severity.
ERP requirement lists SAP, Oracle, NetSuite as examples ('such as'), suggesting experience with any comparable ERP may satisfy it; flagged as bridgeable: maybe.
